channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Over 1,400 passengers already booked seats on Air Zimbabwe’s new Harare-London direct flight – govt says
item.description: SOME 1,479 passengers have already booked their seats on state-owned airline Air Zimbabwe’s new Harare – London direct flight, Transport minister Felix Mhona has revealed. Mhona, who was speaking after receiving the 302-seater Airbus A330-300 aircraft at the Robert Gabriel Mugabe International Airport, also revealed that 30 tonnes of cargo had already been booked and [...] The post Over 1,400 passengers already booked seats on Air Zimbabwe’s new Harare-London direct flight – govt says appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Pensioners drag NSSA to court over ‘peanuts’ payouts
item.description: A group of pensioners has taken the National Social Security Authority (NSSA) to the High Court, accusing the pension body of violating their constitutional rights by paying what they describe as “grossly inadequate” pensions. The applicants, led by Gideon Shoko, are seeking a court order compelling NSSA to urgently review pension payouts to levels they [...] The post Pensioners drag NSSA to court over ‘peanuts’ payouts appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe processes 140,000 fleeing South Africa xenophobia
item.description: APA More than 140,000 nationals from Zimbabwe and Malawi have been repatriated from South Africa through the Beitbridge Border Post since early July, a senior official said on Monday. Chief Director in the Department of Immigration, Respect Gono, told the state-run media that an inter-ministerial committee is on the ground to support a safe and [...] The post Zimbabwe processes 140,000 fleeing South Africa xenophobia appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: 2030 no longer enough – ZANU PF now pushes to extend Mnangagwa’s presidency to 2037 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Barely days after President Emmerson Mnangagwa signed the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Act, 2026 into law, ZANU PF Harare Province has begun pushing to extend his tenure beyond 2030 to 2037. The latest call comes as the ruling party celebrates the enactment of the controversial constitutional amendment, which postpones Zimbabwe’s next harmonised elections [...] The post 2030 no longer enough – ZANU PF now pushes to extend Mnangagwa’s presidency to 2037 appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Unfinished Transition: Why Stability Alone Cannot Deliver Prosperity
item.description: Economic recoveries often succeed twice. The first success is restoring stability. The second, if it comes, is restoring prosperity. The two are related, but they are not the same achievement. Inflation falls, markets begin functioning again, shops fill with goods and consumers regain confidence. After years of instability, these are the changes people naturally celebrate [...] The post Zimbabwe’s Unfinished Transition: Why Stability Alone Cannot Deliver Prosperity appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Harare man sets house on fire, killing two minor children
item.description: A 24-year-old Harare man is under police investigation for murder after allegedly setting fire to a room where his two young children were sleeping following a domestic dispute with their mother. The victims, Aldine Muyengwa (5) and Ropafadzo Muyengwa (2), died in the blaze at Eastview Phase 10, Mabvuku, at around 2:30am on Sunday, according [...] The post Harare man sets house on fire, killing two minor children appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Benjani Junior Mwaruwari Joins Highlanders In Emotional Father-And-Son Reunion
item.description: Benjani Junior Mwaruwari Joins Highlanders Highlanders have confirmed the signing of winger Benjani Junior Mwaruwari, with the 21-year-old joining the Bulawayo giants after playing lower league football in the United Kingdom. The move, announced on 20 July 2026, also creates a unique father-and-son story as the youngster will now play under Highlanders head coach Benjani [...] The post Benjani Junior Mwaruwari Joins Highlanders In Emotional Father-And-Son Reunion appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Air Zim relaunches direct Harare–London flights after 14‐year absence @bulawayo24
item.description: HARARE — Air Zimbabwe today relaunched direct flights between Harare and London, ending a 14‐year absence of a non‐stop air link between Zimbabwe and the United Kingdom and marking what officials desc... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Banks Push For Scrapping Of IMTT As Fresh Warning Emerges
item.description: Bankers Association Says IMTT Is Hurting Formal Business Zimbabwe’s banking sector is urging Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube to abolish the Intermediated Money Transfer Tax (IMTT), arguing that the levy is hurting businesses, discouraging digital payments and slowing economic growth ahead of the Mid-Term Budget Review expected in July 2026. Banks say reducing the tax from [...] The post Banks Push For Scrapping Of IMTT As Fresh Warning Emerges appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: ‘Epworth’s balancing rocks set for tourism boost,’ Tawengwa @bulawayo24
item.description: EPWORTH — Government has moved to position Epworth’s iconic balancing rocks as a major tourism attraction, with Senator Charles Tawengwa, Minister of State for Harare Provincial Affairs and Devolution... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Government Orders Schools To Stop Rejecting Learners Returning From SA
item.description: Zimbabwe Issues New School Order For Learners Returning From South Africa Schools across Zimbabwe have been ordered to stop rejecting learners returning from South Africa after many families fled anti-migrant protests without collecting school reports or transfer letters. The directive comes as the Government intensifies efforts to reintegrate thousands of returning Zimbabweans, including children of [...] The post Government Orders Schools To Stop Rejecting Learners Returning From SA appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Econet Launches New Data Product Amid Persistent Network Complaints @econetzimbabwe
item.description: By Business Reporter | Econet Wireless Zimbabwe has launched a new XtraData Bundles product, promising customers up to twice as much data... Continue reading "Econet Launches New Data Product Amid Persistent Network Complaints" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: RBZ Governor Rules Out Forced Conversion Of US Dollar Savings Into ZiG During Mono-Currency Transition @ReserveBankZIM
item.description: RBZ Governor Clarifies Mono-Currency Plan, Rules Out Forced Conversion Of US Dollar Savings Zimbabweans holding US dollar balances will not be forced to convert their savings into ZiG when the country eventually moves to a mono-currency system, the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) has said. RBZ Governor Dr John Mushayavanhu moved to calm concerns surrounding [...] The post RBZ Governor Rules Out Forced Conversion Of US Dollar Savings Into ZiG During Mono-Currency Transition appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News
